decision: Do not use ElevenLabs Text to Dialogue as the production path. Generate or source small assets, then edit the advert by hand first.
reason: The one-shot take did not separate voices correctly, missed SFX cues, had a sterile tradesperson voice, and ran too long.

editorRecommendation.why: Local multitrack editor with Media Explorer for folder browsing, previewing many candidate sounds, and dragging the chosen asset onto the timeline.
editorRecommendation.secondaryWhy: Good simple local editor, but weaker for browsing and auditioning asset folders inside the creative workflow.
editorRecommendation.avoidForNow: Do not start by building an internal timeline UI. First learn the editing job by doing one advert manually.

assetLibraryRequirement.goal: Peter should open the editor, see sensible asset folders, audition candidates in place, then drag the chosen clip into the timeline.
mustHave: preview or autoplay files before adding them
drag chosen file or selected portion into the timeline
no repeated manual import dialog work
one folder per asset class, such as dog-barks, door, doorbell, beeps, tradesperson-voice, narrator-voice, app-voice

tradesperson.goal: Cheerful real UK tradesperson. Warm, practical, relaxed, not sterile, not actorly.
referenceSearches: Plumberparts James Lawrence conversational plumbing videos
M J Tiff Plumbing tradesman chat in the van
Skill Builder plumbing chat UK tradesperson
tradesperson.rule: Use public videos as vibe references only. Do not clone or imitate a real person's voice without rights and consent.

narrator.goal: Voice most likely to work in a sales advert. Preferred flavour: calm English actress, understated, warm, subtly grown-up.
narrator.rule: Commercial effectiveness first; aesthetic preference second.

appVoice.goal: Separate from narrator. Calm, clean, neutral, useful, lightly synthetic if needed.
appVoice.rule: Should sound like JobDone returning a result, not like the advert narrator.

assets.target: Ordinary house front door, short and clean.
Tiny gadget cue; tells listener this is being captured.

assets.speaker: tradesperson
assets.text: Replaced the kitchen sink washer. Isolation valve is stiff here.
assets.target: Happy chappy talking into phone, pleased with himself.
Can reuse capture_start_beep if distinct beeps are too fussy.

assets.text: small dog bark behind closed door

assets.speaker: tradesperson
assets.text: Oh, and Charlie the dog bites ankles.

assets.speaker: narrator
assets.text: Doodle doo doo. Doodle doo doo. Doodle doo doo. Three months later...
assets.target: Playful original time-jump transition; do not copy a specific copyrighted sting.

assets.speaker: app_voice
assets.text: Results for Mrs Jones. Kitchen sink washer replaced. Isolation valve stiff. Charlie the dog bites ankles.
assets.target: Clean recall result; distinct from narrator.

assets.text: small clean device beep, result found

assets.speaker: tradesperson
assets.target: Hushed, relaxed, confident, almost not a word.

assets.text: small dog bark, footsteps scramble, small kerfuffle as dog steals something
assets.target: Funny fade-out movement, not too busy.

assets.speaker: tradesperson
assets.text: Oi, Charlie! Come back with that.
